skips.reason: The completed depth-two run was not paired with a comparable normalized flat-restart batch in this session, so no state-branch-versus-flat superiority is claimed. Plan WP4 is reported only when both strategies run under comparable measured budgets.

No live capability probe or real process-resident task need is established; the depth-two exploit is a filesystem-class textual delta fully captured by Filesystem Snapshot mode. No Memory adapter scaffold was created.
No kernel-level task need (Docker-in-Sandbox, systemd, eBPF, cgroups, loopback) is established; the task runs under the existing Filesystem Snapshot substrate. No VM adapter scaffold was created.

On this SFT branch, the later QABench wave-40 addendum now provides a preliminary raw-vs-hardened corpus for training-filter analysis, but still no gradient step or transfer claim.

notes: Plan 007 complete: WP1 (independent child re-snapshot), WP2 (one completed live depth-two BranchRun with six branches), and WP3 (live adaptive stop with a real reset-on-new-cluster and a stop-at-four event) are all evidenced; WP4/WP5/WP6 are evidence-backed skips with no unused scaffold.

The depth-two run's confirmed cluster-001 is local to the depth-two node and represents the new /app/test_conftest_demo.py pytest-plugin mechanism discovered at depth-two; it is distinct from Plan 003's cluster-001 (the /app/conftest.py mechanism inherited by the child).

COMMANDS.json keeps plan-007-tests and integration-research not-applicable; those keys are owned by Plan 001, not Plan 007, so they are left unchanged and run as mapped skips (exit 0). Plan 007 validates its tests directly via uv run pytest tests/chronos/research and its integration via the chronos.research.cli resnapshot/depth-two/integration subcommands, all recorded above.

Live execution used the canonical repo .env (auto-loaded by chronos.witnesses.local_env.load_local_env) plus FORKPROOF_ALLOW_EXTERNAL_QA=1; no secret values were read, printed, or committed. Untrusted branch code ran in Modal sandboxes with block_network/outbound allowlist and secrets=[].

Command history was compacted on 2026-06-21T12:58:00Z and superseded by this completed-run record; artifacts/chronos/research/blocked-research-report.json remains as the historical blocked-state snapshot referenced by the living-doc log.

A post-completion parallel-subagent bug review applied three hardening fixes after the live run: the depth-two executor now fails closed (blocked record) instead of crashing if the HUD task/forkpoint load raises; the scheduler concurrency parameter is honored (real in-flight batching, with concurrency=1 byte-identical to the recorded run); and the resnapshot Modal sandbox cleanup is guarded. The recorded depth-two run executed at concurrency=1, whose happy path is unchanged, so the live evidence (commit ffb28b0) is unaltered. Tests grew 30 -> 33.

A second parallel-subagent review added fail-closed robustness: resnapshot now best-effort-terminates the Modal sandbox (a teardown error no longer masks a successful capture) and converts a missing Modal SDK import into a ResnapshotError; the CLI (resnapshot/depth-two/integration) now degrades to a clean STOP (exit 2) on missing or unreadable evidence JSON instead of crashing (exit 1). These touch only error paths; the recorded live run is unchanged. Tests grew 33 -> 35.

A third parallel-subagent bug hunt (concurrency and spec-conformance angles found zero issues; the depth-two branches were confirmed to reference the CHILD snapshot per the REFERENCE). Applied: the integration verifier now checks that a completed depth-two run's branch artifacts resolve to real files on disk and that the child-snapshot lineage/applied-delta and stop-event structure are well-formed (defense-in-depth, not just presence); run_depth_two fails closed on an invalid scheduler config instead of crashing; reward_success_count reuses the canonical reward_success() helper; DepthTwoRunRecord rejects duplicate branch refs and ResearchScheduler rejects an empty node_id. A reviewer suggestion to call modal.Image.from_id() inside the verifier was rejected: it would force live Modal credentials/cost into a cheap local check; snapshot authenticity is established at capture time by resnapshot provenance. The strengthened verifier still accepts the real committed evidence (cli integration exit 0). Recorded live run unchanged. Tests grew 35 -> 39.

A fourth parallel-subagent bug hunt (cross-module-contract angle found zero issues; concurrency angle confirmed the wave loop is race-free). Applied: the verifier now blocks a child-snapshot and depth-two run that reference DIFFERENT child images (catches a re-snapshot inconsistency); build_child_forkpoint deep-copies HUD_TASK_PROFILE's nested lists so the module constant cannot be aliased/corrupted across runs; the frozen record to_record() methods defensively copy mutable nested values; and a previously-untested wave-loop executor-error path is now covered (a raising branch is recorded as executor-error and excluded from completed without stalling the scheduler), alongside SchedulerConfig/complete_branch/non-filesystem/included-paths boundary tests. A reviewer suggestion to block resnapshot re-runs was rejected in favor of the verifier consistency check, which catches the hazard without blocking legitimate re-captures. The strengthened verifier still accepts the real committed evidence (cli integration exit 0); recorded live run unchanged. Tests grew 39 -> 49.

Rebased onto origin/main after Plan 008 (QA classifier benchmark, PR #31) merged (main 61bc582). The only conflict was a single owner-approved Decision-Log relaxation entry in the 007 plan doc, restored verbatim. Plan 008 added disjoint paths (src/chronos/qabench/**) and a uuid run_id in run_live_branch_batch that does not touch _run_one_branch, so Plan 007's implementation (src/chronos/research/**) is byte-identical and all 49 tests pass. One test needed a precise fix: the research-isolation check now matches real `import chronos.research` statements instead of any substring, because Plan 008's sft_export.py docstring merely names a research symbol without importing it (research stays deletable; invariant intact). validate_graph now reports 10 dependencies (008's Plan 005 edge owner-relaxed) and one Plan-007 test file is 7 lines over the 500 target (within the 550 max). --require-complete still passes.

SFT branch addendum: wave-40 QABench collection used post-agent Modal filesystem snapshots plus sterile clean_verify with HUD QA skipped for speed. Landed 9 count=4 task reports: 36 discovered branches, 27 reward-positive/adjudicated trajectories, 11 confirmed hacks, 16 sterile verifier-legit, and 0 undecided. The conservative SFT pipeline exported 27 raw positives, 3 certified SFT-clean positives, 11 rejected hacks, and 13 quarantined records. Combined with the original Plan 008 QABench partition, the branch has 21 confirmed reward-hack trajectories deduped to 14 distinct task+mechanism clusters; wave40 contributes 6 net-new distinct clusters beyond the original partition. Evidence is in docs/plans/evidence/007/SFT-WAVE40.md and artifacts/chronos/research/sft/runs/qabench_wave40/.
